maml:description.maml:para: Adds a structured finding to the in-memory collection.
Phase 0 helper. Called automatically by Write-Success/DiagWarning/DiagError so existing checks contribute to JSON/CSV/SARIF output without rewriting.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Captures diagnostic output from a script block and offers to save and open it
The diagnostic function to capture output from
The diagnostic function to capture output from

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Serialises $script:Findings to JSON, NDJSON, CSV, or SARIF-lite.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a comprehensive system diagnostic report
Creates a detailed text report with system information, resource usage, and configuration details

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Export-SystemReport

maml:description.maml:para: Exports TLS configuration to a report file
Creates a detailed report of TLS settings for documentation

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Detects cluster membership, role, and SQL AG state for the local node
Returns a hashtable with IsClusterNode, ClusterName, NodeName, IsAGInstalled, AGReplicas, and ClusterNetworks information. All downstream checks should use this cached result.

dev:type.maml:name: Hashtable with cluster and AG environment details

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Gets installed .NET Framework and .NET Core versions
Scans registry and outputs all installed versions of .NET framework

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Safely extracts a message snippet from an event log entry
Maximum number of characters to return (default: 100)
Maximum number of characters to return (default: 100)

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Returns a snapshot copy of current findings.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Returns default gateways excluding cluster heartbeat-only networks

Array of adapter names that are cluster heartbeat-only

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Returns a cached PSCustomObject describing the host's OS, edition, architecture, role inventory and Server-2025-feature flags.
Replaces scattered Get-CimInstance Win32_OperatingSystem / Get-WindowsFeature calls. Safe on workstation, Core, ARM64. Idempotent / cached.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Gets top processes by CPU and Memory usage in a single call
Number of top processes to return (default: 10)
Number of top processes to return (default: 10)

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Shared helper to query recent Windows Event Log entries
Event log name (e.g. System, Application, Security)
Array of Event IDs to search for
Number of hours to look back (default: 24)
Number of days to look back (overrides HoursBack if specified)
Number of days to look back (overrides HoursBack if specified)
Array of Event IDs to search for
Number of hours to look back (default: 24)
Event log name (e.g. System, Application, Security)

maml:description.maml:para: Invokes a TSS command with proper path handling
The TSS command to execute (without the .\TSS.ps1 prefix)
The TSS command to execute (without the .\TSS.ps1 prefix)

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Invoke-TSSCommand "-SDP Net -AcceptEula"

maml:description.maml:para: Executes a command with TSS availability check

Script block to execute if TSS is not available

command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Main entry point for the troubleshooting tool
Initializes the tool, checks prerequisites, and displays the main menu

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Start-TroubleshootingTool Start-TroubleshootingTool -EnableLogging

maml:description.maml:para: Starts Windows Update related log collection
Provides options for Windows Update trace and CBS log collection

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: DC-only health audit (dcdiag, repadmin, FSMO, SYSVOL, time, krbtgt, LDAP signing).

command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: S2D / Storage Replica / Dedup / ReFS / Storage QoS / NVMe firmware checks.

command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Arc agent health, AMA, outbound endpoints, extension state.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Expiring certs (My + WebHosting), WinRM listener cert, chain build, private key ACL audit.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Checks current CPU usage, per-core hotspots, privileged vs user time, queue length, context switches, interrupts/DPCs, power throttling, process analysis, AV detection, Hyper-V overhead, NUMA, and CPU events

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-CPUUsage

maml:description.maml:para: Consolidated health scorecard surfacing highest-frequency cross-cutting issues
Quick summary of the most common production issues across all categories: cluster/AG instability, storage errors, DNS failures, RDP connectivity

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Checks physical disks, logical disk space, latency, cluster size, IOPS, throughput, media type, SMART health, VSS snapshots, Storage Spaces, fragmentation, pagefile placement, filter drivers, MPIO, ReFS/NTFS, disk busy time, and storage tiering

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-DiskPerformance

maml:description.maml:para: Verifies DNS server configuration, tests resolution, cache stats, and service status

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-DNSHealth

maml:description.maml:para: Analyzes Windows Event Logs for issues
Scans System and Application logs for recent errors, groups by EventID, and checks log capacity

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-EventLogHealth

maml:description.maml:para: Hyper-V host audit (VMs, integration services, dynamic memory, checkpoints, replica, vSwitch, GPU-P).

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Checks IIS services, AppPools, Websites, and Worker Processes.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Checks total memory, usage percentage, top consumers, committed bytes, NonPaged/Paged pool, page file, compression, handles/threads, standby cache, RAM hardware, resource exhaustion events, WS trimming, and leak detection

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-MemoryUsage

maml:description.maml:para: Audits VBS/HVCI/Credential Guard, LSA-PPL, ASR, BitLocker, LAPS, SMB1, SMB signing, Defender.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Checks RSS status, ephemeral port usage, VMQ settings, adapter properties, power plan, network statistics, default gateway, link speed/duplex, TCP offload, MTU/jumbo frames, DNS suffix, WINS, proxy, NIC drivers, binding order, firewall rules, RDMA/SMB Direct, TCP parameters, NIC error events, and routing table

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-NetworkConfiguration

maml:description.maml:para: Hotpatch state, latest LCU age, pending-reboot root cause, failed updates, OS lifecycle.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Checks if a given path resides on a Cluster Shared Volume
The file system path to validate
Array of CSV mount points from Get-ClusterEnvironmentInfo
Array of CSV mount points from Get-ClusterEnvironmentInfo
The file system path to validate

dev:type.maml:name: Boolean - $true if path is on a CSV

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Validates and optionally creates a path
If specified, creates the path if it doesn't exist
If specified, creates the path if it doesn't exist

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Examines account lockout policies, recent failed logons, Kerberos status, secure channel, and firewall profiles

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-SecurityAuthentication

maml:description.maml:para: Detection of Hotpatch, dMSA, NTLM deprecation, SMB-over-QUIC, NetATC, GPU-P, ARM64, OpenSSH, TLS 1.3.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Checks critical services, stopped automatic services, and recently failed services

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-ServicesHealth

maml:description.maml:para: Checks failed tasks, long-running tasks, disabled tasks, high-privilege tasks, credential failures, SDDL permissions, orphaned executables, and trigger health

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-TaskSchedulerHealth

maml:description.maml:para: Validates TLS configuration on the server
Checks enabled TLS protocols, cipher suites, and security configurations

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-TLSConfiguration

maml:description.maml:para: Checks if TSS is available at the configured path
Verifies TSS.ps1 exists at the configured path

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Checks Windows Update status and history
Shows recent updates, pending reboot status, days since last update, and Windows Update service status

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Test-WindowsUpdateStatus

maml:description.maml:para: Wrapper for `Get-Command -EA SilentlyContinue` used by the new options to gracefully N/A when a cmdlet is missing on the running OS.
